seed_problem: K non-crossing quay cranes on one rail must be assigned M positioned container jobs and staggered in time; minimize makespan plus idle standby energy.
mutation: composite: compose the non-crossing (monotone matching) feasibility rule, a heterogeneous crane-to-zone matching (standby powers), and a temporal-staggering rule (congested stretches force serialized busy intervals) into one coupled makespan+idle-energy objective; the naive load-balancing greedy cuts through congested blobs and pays serialization it never modelled.
theme: container cranes on one shared rail unloading a ship
title: Quay Cranes on One Rail: Zone Partition and Handoff Staggering
strategies: even-count baseline: split position-sorted jobs into K equal-count contiguous zones, cranes 1..K, minimal staggered offsets (matches the checker baseline, ratio ~0.1)

load-balancing greedy: cut so each crane gets (almost) equal total hoisting work, ignoring the safety gaps and standby powers; on blob instances this slices congested clusters and forces long staggering

gap-aware partition + power matching: place zone boundaries at wide (>=S) bay gaps so nothing serializes, choose which cranes to deploy so high-standby-power cranes get the busiest zones, then local-search the cuts against the true objective
local search over cut positions and deployed-crane subset evaluating the exact makespan+idle cost
